name: Speedmaster
description: Identify the Speedmaster ('Moonwatch') by its three sub-dials on a black dial, the tachymetre scale on the bezel, and straight lugs with twisted edges. It may come in variants like: black 'Moonwatch' dial, white dial, and the Snoopy editions.

name: Seamaster Diver 300M
description: Identify the Seamaster Diver 300M by its wave-patterned dial, skeletonised 'sword' hands, a date window, and the helium-escape valve at 10 o'clock on the case side. It may come in variants like: blue dial-and-bezel (James Bond), black, and grey.

name: Aqua Terra
description: Identify the Aqua Terra by its clean dial with vertical 'teak' stripes that echo teak decking, and a simple polished bezel — dressier than the diver Seamasters. It may come in variants like: blue dial, silver dial, and small-seconds versions.

name: Planet Ocean
description: Identify the Planet Ocean by its bolder, chunkier diver case, a colour-accented rotating bezel (often orange or red), broad arrow-tipped hands, and a prominent date. It may come in variants like: orange-bezel, blue-bezel, and black-on-black 'Deep Black'.

name: Seamaster 300
description: Identify the vintage-styled Seamaster 300 by its faux-patina cream lume, broad arrow hands, and a sandwich dial under a matte rotating bezel evoking the 1957 original. It may come in variants like: black dial and blue dial.

name: Railmaster
description: Identify the Railmaster by its very plain anti-magnetic field-watch dial: a matte sandwich dial, vintage-cream baton numerals, a railway-track minute ring, and no rotating bezel. It may come in variants like: grey dial and faux-patina black dial.

name: Constellation
description: Identify the Constellation by the four 'griffe' claws gripping the bezel at 3 and 9 o'clock, the integrated bracelet, a Roman-numeral bezel, and the star emblem on the dial. It may come in variants like: silver dial, blue dial, and two-tone gold.

name: Globemaster
description: Identify the Globemaster by its fluted 'pie-pan' dial that steps down towards the edge, a fluted bezel, and a small star at 6 o'clock. It may come in variants like: silver pie-pan dial, blue dial, and sun-brushed grey.

name: De Ville
description: Identify the De Ville by its classic round dress case, slim profile, and clean dial often with Roman numerals — Omega's most formal, non-sporty line. It may come in variants like: silver dial, blue dial, and the Trésor and Prestige sub-lines.
